expanded_papers.title: Real-Time State-Of-Charge Estimation for Electric Vehicle Batteries Using Driving Pattern Synchronized Neural Networks
expanded_papers.abstract: Digital transformation has opened new opportunities and innovations in several industries, especially in the transportation sector with the help of information and communication technology assisted with artificial intelligence and industrial IoT (IIoT). Hence our research focuses primarily on the development of the digital twin of the Battery Management Systems (BMS) for lead acid batteries and enhance its functionality and intelligence. We propose PyBMS, a novel and enhanced BMS digital twin framework that leverages a driving-pattern-synchronised long short-term memory network combined with parallel rectified filtration to enable real-time estimation of the State-of-Charge (SoC) and Performance Index. The system dynamically identifies current driving patterns and detects battery pack anomalies, further improving accuracy through the integration of the Voltage-independent Extended Kalman Filter (V-EKF) algorithm - a novel advancement over the conventional Extended Kalman Filter (EKF).

expanded_papers.title: Research on an Improved PCB Defect Detection Method Based on the YOLOv8 Algorithm
expanded_papers.abstract: As the core component of modern electronic industry, the defect detection accuracy of printed circuit boards (PCBs) directly affects the system reliability in key fields such as high-speed communication, industrial control, and artificial intelligence. Aiming at the problems of small size, diverse shapes, and low background contrast in PCBs, an improved DEBCNYOL08s deep learning detection model is proposed. This model improves detection performance in two aspects. Firstly, a D _ E-C2f module is designed to replace the C2f structure of YOLOv8s, integrating detail enhancement and multi-scale perception mechanisms to significantly enhance the ability to capture small defect features; Secondly, the loss function of the model was optimized by dynamically fusing normalized Wasserstein distance and complete intersection to union ratio (Clo U), effectively alleviating the impact of target size differences and localization biases on detection. The experiment showed that the model achieved 98.7% mAP on a self built dataset. Meanwhile, an integrated detection system has been developed based on PyQt5 and OpenCV, which supports one click defect localization and visual analysis, significantly reducing the threshold for industrial deployment.

expanded_papers.title: MSI-YOLO: An Improved YOLOv8n Algorithm for PCB Surface Defect Detection
expanded_papers.abstract: Defect detection is a critical step in ensuring the quality of printed circuit boards (PCBs). PCB surface defects typically exhibit characteristics such as low resolution, small targets, and high diversity, making defect detection on PCB surfaces highly challenging. To improve the accuracy of PCB surface defect detection, this study proposes an improved MSI-YOLO algorithm based on the YOLOv8n model. First, to address the issue of insufficient feature discrimination in the backbone network stage, this paper introduces a multiscale feature enhancement module (MFEM) designed to reduce background interference while enhancing relevant features. In addition, to address the issue of excessive feature compression in the Spatial Pyramid Pooling Fast (SPPF) module of the backbone network, this paper proposes a spatial feature pyramid module (SFPM). This module not only maintains the construction of multi-scale receptive fields but also enhances the ability to extract local features. Finally, to enrich the global contextual information contained in the detection features, this study introduces an improved spatial context-aware module (ISCAM). On the publicly available DeepPCB dataset, the MSI-YOLO model achieved an accuracy of 98.6%, a recall rate of 97.5%, an mAP50 of 98.9%, and an mAP75 of 93.4% and an mAP50-95 of 81.6%, outperforming other detection models. The MSI-YOLO model, while maintaining a lightweight design, significantly enhances the detection accuracy of PCB surface defects and demonstrates excellent deployment adaptability.

expanded_papers.title: Vision-Language RAG for Post-AOI PCB Inspection
expanded_papers.abstract: This study introduces an AOI advisory system utilizing a multimodal Large Language Model (LLM) augmented with Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) for PCB defect detection. Operating as a post-inspection "second opinion" tool, it interprets complex results and generates explainable quality reports by correlating with historical defect data. Our approach, unlike traditional image classification, leverages RAG and vision-language models to provide context-rich, reference-backed justifications, aiming to reduce false calls and enhance traceability. Experiments show that RAG improves the accuracy in identifying multiple co-occurring defects, achieving 37.04% compared to 33.33% for the No-RAG approach at prompt level 1, and 77.78% compared to 44.44% for the No-RAG approach at prompt level 6. These findings suggest that RAG significantly enhances LLM performance in complex PCB inspection, offering a pathway beyond reliance on detailed prompt engineering.

expanded_papers.title: Vision transformer and Mamba-attention fusion for high-precision PCB defect detection
expanded_papers.abstract: Defects in printed circuit boards (PCBs) are being detected using computer vision-based techniques. Defect-free PCBs are essential for the reliability of consumer electronics. However, deep learning-based methods often struggle with imbalanced defect distributions and limited generalization. To address these challenges, we propose ViT-Mamba, a hybrid framework that combines Vision Transformers with a Mamba-inspired attention mechanism for global feature extraction and precise defect segmentation. We further introduce an artificial defect generation module that systematically creates six types of PCB defects to improve robustness. A multiscale hierarchical refinement strategy is employed to enhance feature representation for accurate segmentation. Experiments on a public PCB defect dataset show that ViT-Mamba outperforms existing methods, achieving a mean Average Precision (mAP) of 99.69%.

expanded_papers.title: Rapid visual detection of laser welding defects in bright stainless steel thin plates
expanded_papers.abstract: Stainless steel sheets with bright surfaces have been widely used in household appliances, decorative materials, highway and railway vehicles, and other fields. In order to reduce welding deformation while maintaining overall aesthetics, the commonly used welding method is laser welding. In laser welding, various welding defects are the main causes of weld failure. At present, the main detection method for defects in such slender welds is laser structured light visual scanning detection. However, due to the fact that the reflection of high gloss stainless steel material is mainly mirror reflection, there will be a substantial amount of signal loss in structured light visual inspection. This article proposes a characterization and detection method for the surface morphology and defects of laser welding of high reflective materials. The method uses latitude and longitude RGB light sources for illumination, which can form distinct and different forms of rainbow bars on various types of weld defects, providing powerful conditions for defect recognition. Unlike laser structured light vision, this method fully utilizes the specular reflection characteristics of the material surface and has a faster detection speed. This article uses a ball model to reveal the decoupled correspondence between material surface color and gradient direction and magnitude under latitude-distributed and longitude-distributed RGB light source illumination, explaining the mechanism and reliability of the detection method, and providing conditions for further quantification of defects. Finally, this article combines U-Net network and its various improved versions to achieve high-precision welding defect detection, with an average accuracy of 89.88% and a recall rate of 92%.

expanded_papers.title: Improved THT Solder Joint in PCB Defect Detection Model Based on YOLOv8
expanded_papers.abstract: Detection of solder joint defects on printed circuit boards is an important means of improving the production quality of printed circuit boards. In this study, improvements are made to the YOLOv8 model by restructuring the neck network architecture of YOLOv8s through the BiFPN network. This modification reduces the number of parameters of the model while improving its multi-scale feature detection capability. To address the issue of sample imbalance in the target detection dataset for THT solder joint defects. Equalization Loss is introduced to adjust the weights of the samples, thereby improving the detection capability for defect categories. Finally, the improved model is compared with the yolov8s model, an improvement of 2.2% in average precision mAP@0.5:0.95, with a 33.78% reduction in the number of parameters.

stitching_steps: 1. 在Python+OpenCV环境下复现baseline的图像减法流程：读取标准图像与待测图像，进行SIFT配准，计算绝对差分，阈值分割得到候选区域。

计算资源需求增加，需GPU支持Autoencoder推理。

research_narrative.abstract_draft: 针对PCB缺陷检测中精度与速度难以兼顾的问题，本文提出一种基于机器视觉的混合检测系统。首先利用图像减法快速定位缺陷候选区域，再通过轻量级CNN进行精确分类；同时引入基于参考图像的无监督变化检测方法，增强对微小缺陷的敏感度。此外，将传统图像处理特征与YOLO-pdd的多尺度检测能力融合，进一步提升复杂背景下的检测精度。在DeepPCB数据集上的实验表明，该方法在保持实时性的同时，检测精度优于现有主流方法，为工业PCB缺陷检测提供了高效解决方案。
